Examples:

```pycon
>>> import lamindb as ln
>>> ln.setup.migrate.create()
>>> ln.setup.migrate.deploy()
>>> ln.setup.migrate.check()
```
for tests, see lamin-cli

modules.name: lamindb_setup
documentation:
Setup & configure LaminDB.

Many functions in this "setup API" have a matching command in the {doc}`docs:cli` CLI.

Guide: {doc}`docs:setup`.

Basic operations:

```
.. autosummary::
   :toctree:

   login
   logout
   init
   close
   delete
```

Instance operations:

```
.. autosummary::
   :toctree:

   migrate
```

Modules & settings:

```
.. autosummary::
   :toctree:

   settings
   core
   django
```

variables.name: lamindb_setup.__version__
documentation: denote a release candidate for 0.1.0 with 0.1rc1

functions.name: lamindb_setup._django.django
documentation:
Manage migrations.

Examples:

Reset auto-incrementing primary integer ids after a database import:

```pycon
>>> import lamindb as ln
>>> ln.setup.django("sqlsequencereset", "lnschema_core")
BEGIN;
SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('"lnschema_core_user"','id'), coalesce(max("id"), 1), max("id") IS NOT null) FROM "lnschema_core_user";  # noqa
SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('"lnschema_core_storage"','id'), coalesce(max("id"), 1), max("id") IS NOT null) FROM "lnschema_core_storage";  # noqa
COMMIT;
```

You can then run the SQL output that you'll see like so:

```pycon
>>> sql = \"\"\"BEGIN;
    SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('"lnschema_core_user"','id'), coalesce(max("id"), 1), max("id") IS NOT null) FROM "lnschema_core_user";  # noqa
    SELECT setval(pg_get_serial_sequence('"lnschema_core_storage"','id'), coalesce(max("id"), 1), max("id") IS NOT null) FROM "lnschema_core_storage";  # noqa
    COMMIT;\"\"\"
>>> from django.db import connection
>>> with connection.cursor() as cursor:
        cursor.execute(sql)
```

functions.name: lamindb_setup._delete.delete
documentation:
Delete a LaminDB instance.

Args:
    slug: The instance slug `account_handle/instance_name` or URL.
        If the instance is owned by you, it suffices to pass the instance name.
    force: Whether to skip the confirmation prompt.
    require_empty: Whether to check if the instance is empty before deleting.

functions.name: lamindb_setup._init_instance.init
documentation:
Create and load a LaminDB instance.

Args:

: storage: Either `"create-s3"`, local or

  : remote folder (`"s3://..."` or `"gs://..."`).

  name: Instance name.
  db: Database connection url, do not pass for SQLite.
  schema: Comma-separated string of schema modules. None if not set.

functions.name: lamindb_setup._connect_instance.connect
documentation:
Connect to instance.

Args:
    slug: The instance slug `account_handle/instance_name` or URL.
        If the instance is owned by you, it suffices to pass the instance name.

functions.name: lamindb_setup._connect_instance.load
documentation:
Connect to instance and set `auto-connect` to true.

This is exactly the same as `ln.connect()` except for that
`ln.connect()` doesn't change the state of `auto-connect`.

functions.name: lamindb_setup._connect_instance.migrate_lnschema_bionty

```
param db_uri

str, database URI (e.g., 'sqlite:///path/to/db.sqlite' or 'postgresql://user:password@host:port/dbname')
```

documentation: The directory that holds locally persisted settings.

methods.name: lamindb_setup.core._settings.SetupSettings.auto_connect
documentation:
Auto-connect to current instance upon `import lamindb`.

Upon installing `lamindb`, this setting is `False`.

Upon calling `lamin init` or `lamin connect` on the CLI, this setting is switched to `True`.

`ln.connect()` doesn't change the value of this setting.

You can manually change this setting

- in Python: `ln.setup.settings.auto_connect = True/False`
- via the CLI: `lamin settings set auto-connect true/false`

methods.name: lamindb_setup.core._settings.SetupSettings.private_django_api
documentation:
Turn internal Django API private to clean up the API (default `False`).

This patches your local pip-installed django installation. You can undo
the patch by setting this back to `False`.

modules.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath
documentation: Paths & file systems.
we are not documenting UPath here because it's documented at lamindb.UPath

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.download_to
documentation: Download from self (a destination in the cloud) to the local path.

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.upload_from
documentation:
Upload from the local path to `self` (a destination in the cloud).

If the local path is a directory, recursively upload its contents.

Args:
    local_path: A local path of a file or directory.
    create_folder: Only applies if `local_path` is a directory and then
        defaults to `True`. If `True`, make a new folder in the destination
        using the directory name of `local_path`. If `False`, upload the
        contents of the directory to to the root-level of the destination.
    print_progress: Print progress.

Returns:
    The destination path.

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.synchronize
documentation: Sync to a local destination path.

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.modified
documentation: Return modified time stamp.

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.view_tree
documentation:
Print a visual tree structure of files & directories.

Args:
    level: If `1`, only iterate through one level, if `2` iterate through 2
        levels, if `-1` iterate through entire hierarchy.
    only_dirs: Only iterate through directories.
    n_max_files: Display limit. Will only show this many files. Doesn't affect count.
    include_paths: Restrict to these paths.
    skip_suffixes: Skip directories with these suffixes.

Examples:
    >>> dir_path = ln.core.datasets.generate_cell_ranger_files(
    >>>     "sample_001", ln.settings.storage
    >>> )
    >>> ln.UPath(dir_path).view_tree()
    3 subdirectories, 15 files
    sample_001
    ├── web_summary.html
    ├── metrics_summary.csv
    ├── molecule_info.h5
    ├── filtered_feature_bc_matrix
    │   ├── features.tsv.gz
    │   ├── barcodes.tsv.gz
    │   └── matrix.mtx.gz
    ├── analysis
    │   └── analysis.csv
    ├── raw_feature_bc_matrix
    │   ├── features.tsv.gz
    │   ├── barcodes.tsv.gz
    │   └── matrix.mtx.gz
    ├── possorted_genome_bam.bam.bai
    ├── cloupe.cloupe
    ├── possorted_genome_bam.bam
    ├── filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5
    └── raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5
adapted from: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9727673

functions.name: lamindb_setup.core.upath.to_url
documentation:
Public storage URL.

Generates a public URL for an object in an S3 bucket using fsspec's UPath,
considering the bucket's region.

Args:
- upath: A UPath object representing an S3 path.

Returns:
- A string containing the public URL to the S3 object.
